message:
feat(linalg): pinv hermitian=True semantics with lower-triangle-only symmetrization (franken_numpy-2job)

NumPy's np.linalg.pinv(a, hermitian=True) skips the generic SVD path and goes
through eigh, which means it only reads the lower triangle of the input and
ignores whatever garbage is in the upper triangle. Callers rely on that to
pass asymmetric working buffers as long as the lower triangle is the "real"
matrix they care about. Our existing pinv_nxn has no such door, so Python-side
callers using hermitian=True could not get parity.

New public API in fnp-linalg:

  pinv_hermitian_nxn(a, n, rcond) -> Result<Vec<f64>, LinAlgError>
  pinv_hermitian_nxn_with_tolerance_aliases(a, n, rcond, rtol)
     -> Result<Vec<f64>, LinAlgError>

Implementation notes:

- hermitian_from_lower_triangle rebuilds a fully symmetric view from a[row*n+col]
  for col <= row, so any values a caller left in the strict upper triangle are
  discarded before we compute eigenvalues. This matches NumPy byte-for-byte on
  asymmetric inputs whose lower triangle is the canonical form.

- Eigen-decomposition via eigh_nxn, cutoff via pinv_singular_value_cutoff
  (same sigma_max*rcond rule the non-hermitian path uses), then inversion of
  each eigenvalue above the cutoff and reconstruction as V diag(1/λ) Vᵀ.
  Shape/finite-input guards follow the same error taxonomy as pinv_nxn.

- tolerance-aliases wrapper routes through the shared
  resolve_pinv_tolerance_aliases helper so rcond/rtol precedence and
  "both supplied" rejection stay consistent with the generic pinv path.

Tests:

- pinv_hermitian_uses_lower_triangle_like_numpy: deliberately passes
  [[2,4],[1,2]] — non-symmetric upper — and asserts we recover the
  closed-form pinv of the *symmetric* [[2,1],[1,2]] matrix (2/3, -1/3;
  -1/3, 2/3), proving the upper triangle really is ignored. Second case
  locks the rcond=1e-9 behavior on near-singular eigenvalues.

- pinv_hermitian_matches_live_numpy_oracle_when_available: new
  numpy_oracle_pinv_hermitian helper that shells out to the oracle Python
  with hermitian=True and all combinations of (rcond_set/omitted,
  rtol_set/none/omitted) aliases. Diff-checked against our Rust output so
  any future drift in tolerance-alias resolution or singular-value cutoff
  surfaces immediately against the real NumPy build.

Registers franken_numpy-2job (in_progress) in the beads snapshot.
